This biographical article relating to Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Sidra_Hamad&action=edit).

(born 3 December 1986) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) She was the bronze medalist at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the women's doubles event,[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the runner-up at the [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

["Players: Sidra Hamad"](http://bwfbadminton.com/player/72028/sidra-hamad). *bwfbadminton.com*. [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation"). Retrieved 12 November 2016.

["Sidra Hamad Full Profile"](http://bwf.tournamentsoftware.com/profile/biography.aspx?id=2A2B5BE4-0C9F-476E-B0CC-E0C0032DE3BF). *bwf.tournamentsoftware.com*. [Badminton World Federation](/wiki/Badminton_World_Federation "Badminton World Federation"). Retrieved 12 November 2016.

["XII South Asian Games"](http://www.sports.gov.pk/Participation/12th_SA_Games.html). *www.sports.gov.pk*. [Pakistan Sports Board](/wiki/Pakistan_Sports_Board "Pakistan Sports Board"). Retrieved 16 December 2019.

["South Asian Games : Ruthvika Shivani Upset PV Sindhu To Win Gold"](http://xtratime.in/south-asian-games-ruthvika-shivani-upset-pv-sindhu-to-win-gold/). *xtratime.in*. 10 February 2016. Retrieved 16 December 2019.

["Rizwan, Palwasha win badminton titles"](https://www.thenews.com.pk/print/159018-Rizwan-Palwasha-win-badminton-titles). *www.thenews.com.pk*. [The News International](/wiki/The_News_International "The News International"). Retrieved 12 November 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player (born 1986)

Sidra Hamad

**Sidra Hamad** (born 3 December 1986) is a Pakistani [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) She was the bronze medalist at the [2016 South Asian Games](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2016_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2016 South Asian Games") in the women's doubles event,[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the runner-up at the [Pakistan International](/wiki/Pakistan_International_(badminton) "Pakistan International (badminton)") tournament.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
